item.title: 21 Code of Conduct
item.description: Adapted from the Contributor Covenant, version 2.1 TL;DR Be kind, be respectful, be professional.

item.title: 22 About
item.description: Why does CAPIBARA exist? The Collaboration for the Analysis of Photonic and Ionic Bursts and Radiation (CAPIBARA) was established in July 2024 with a clear mandate: To explore the high-energy cosmos To do so with multi-messenger analysis To be fully led by students CAPIBARA’s development was acceler...

item.title: 23 Contributing Guidelines
item.description: ABSTRACT The goal of this page is to master the GitHub Workflow. In a scientific collaboration, GitHub is more than a code host.

item.title: 24 CAPIBARA Handbook
item.description: The Name CAPIBARA or CAPIBARA Collaboration stands for Collaboration for the Analysis of Photonic and Ionic Bursts and Radiation. 1.

item.title: Full Privacy Policy
item.description: Version 1.0 | Effective Date: January 1, 2026 About This Policy The CAPIBARA Collaboration is a small, informal student research group.

item.title: README
item.description: What is the GAMMA Initiative? TODO What is our current focus? We are currently studying and conducting research to motivate a \gamma-ray transient observing student CubeSat mission.

item.title: 16b Python Basics
item.description: ABSTRACT Python is the “Swiss Army Knife” of modern science.

item.title: 01 Applying to Join
item.description: Summary This page explains how to join the CAPIBARA Collaboration as a student at any level.

item.title: 11 Unix
item.description: ABSTRACT The goal of this coding tutorial is to set up a unix-like environment on your computer.

item.title: 12 Terminal
item.description: ABSTRACT The Terminal (also called Command Line or Shell) is a text-based way to talk to your computer.
